description: A retried or duplicated operation applies its side effect twice because there is no uniqueness guard.
body: Failure mode: an operation with a side effect (charge, increment, send, insert) can run concurrently or be retried, and a check-then-act guard lets two callers both pass, so the effect is applied twice. Detection signals: duplicate charges/messages under retry or load; counts higher than expected; the bug appears only when the same request arrives twice quickly. Do not rely on check-then-act under concurrency. Safer fallback: derive the effect from a unique operation key with an atomic insert-if-absent or compare-and-set, dedupe at the boundary, and verify with a concurrent test firing the same key many times that the effect lands exactly once.
